title: NETWORKDAYS
summary: NETWORKDAYS counts the number of working days between two dates, automatically excluding weekends and any holidays you supply.
jobUseCases: Calculate business days a support ticket stayed open for an SLA report
Work out working days until a project deadline for resource planning
Count chargeable working days in a month for a consultant's timesheet
Measure invoice payment turnaround in business days for finance
Plan delivery dates that skip weekends and public holidays in operations

examples.title: Working days between two dates
examples.explanation: With a start date in B2 and end date in C2, this returns the count of Monday-to-Friday days between them, inclusive of both ends. A Mon-to-Fri span returns 5; it ignores Saturday and Sunday automatically.

examples.title: Excluding public holidays
examples.explanation: Pass a range of holiday dates (e.g. Diwali, Republic Day) as the third argument. Any holiday that falls on a weekday is subtracted, so the result reflects true working days, which is essential for accurate SLA tracking.

examples.title: Business days elapsed so far
examples.explanation: Using TODAY() as the end date gives a live count of working days a ticket has been open up to now, minus listed holidays. Wrapping it as part of an aging report keeps SLA breach flags current.

examples.title: Custom weekend with NETWORKDAYS.INTL
examples.explanation: When the work week is not Mon-Fri, use NETWORKDAYS.INTL. The weekend code 7 means Friday-Saturday off (common in some regions), and you can still pass a holidays range. The base NETWORKDAYS always assumes Sat-Sun.

commonMistakes: Assuming NETWORKDAYS is exclusive; it actually counts both the start and end dates when they are weekdays
Using NETWORKDAYS for a non-standard work week instead of NETWORKDAYS.INTL, getting wrong weekend handling
Listing holidays as text rather than real date values, so they are not excluded
Forgetting to lock the holidays range ($H$2:$H$10) so it shifts when the formula is copied down
Expecting a negative result when end is before start; the function returns a negative count, which can surprise users
practiceTips: Keep a maintained holidays list (named range) and reference it across all your business-day formulas.
Use NETWORKDAYS.INTL when your weekend is not Saturday-Sunday or varies by country.
Pair with WORKDAY to do the reverse: find a date that is N working days after a start date.
Format inputs as true dates; if they look right but the count is off, check for text-formatted dates.
